title: Largest completed order per customer
prompt: For each customer with at least one completed order, return their single largest completed order: columns `name`, `order_id`, `amount` — exactly one row per customer (their highest-amount completed order; on a tie pick the lowest order_id). Order by `amount` descending, then `name`.

generic: JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id
QUALIFY ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY c.customer_id ORDER BY o.amount DESC, o.order_id) = 1

hints: Rank each customer’s completed orders by amount with ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY …).
Keep only rank 1. DuckDB supports QUALIFY to filter on a window function directly.
